content:
# Disagreements — Genesis 36 (GEN_036)

## 1. bat-bat Construction — Literal vs. Interpretive (vv. 2, 14)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "daughter of Anah, daughter of Zibeon" | Literal double bat preserved |
| LOGOS | "daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon" | Literal; context clarifies |
| PNEUMA | "granddaughter of Zibeon" | Interpretive; clarifies genealogy for modern reader |
| **SOPHIA** | **Literal double "daughter" — 2-1** | The Hebrew syntax is the translator's responsibility. Context (vv. 20, 24) makes the genealogical relationship clear. LXX reads "son of Zibeon" — a different resolution. |

## 2. Yemim — "Hot springs" vs. Transliteration (v. 24)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "hot springs" with uncertainty note | Vulgate tradition (aquas calidas); best-attested interpretation |
| LOGOS | "yemim" (transliterated) | True hapax; meaning genuinely unknown; transliteration preserves obscurity |
| PNEUMA | "hot springs" | Vulgate/scholarly consensus; concrete narrative detail |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Hot springs" — 2-1** | LOGOS's caution is noted, but the Vulgate tradition provides a coherent reading. Transliteration leaves the reader without any interpretive foothold. |

## 3. "Struck" vs. "Defeated" Midian (v. 35)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "who struck Midian" | Literal: hakkeh = "the one striking" (participle of nakah) |
| LOGOS | "who struck Midian" | Preserves the physical violence of nakah |
| PNEUMA | "who defeated Midian" | Dynamic: communicates the outcome |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Who struck Midian" — 2-1** | nakah denotes the act of striking, not the result. "Defeated" over-interprets. |

## 4. Mippenei — "Away from the face of" vs. "Away from" (v. 6)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "away from the face of Jacob his brother" | Preserves Hebrew idiom mippenei literally |
| LOGOS | "away from his brother Jacob" | Natural English; "from the face of" is Hebraism |
| PNEUMA | "away from his brother Jacob" | Dynamic equivalent |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Away from his brother Jacob" — 2-1** | The Hebraism "from the face of" does not add meaning in English. Available in EMET's literal version. |

## 5. "He is Edom" — Rendering of hu' Edom (vv. 1, 8, 19, 43)
| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "he is Edom" | Literal: hu = "he" |
| LOGOS | "that is, Edom" (v.1) / "Esau is Edom" (v.8) | Varies by context; appositional rendering |
| PNEUMA | "that is, Edom" (v.1) / "Esau is Edom" (v.8) | Same as LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **Context-dependent: "that is, Edom" for appositional (v.1), "Esau is Edom" for declarative (v.8)** | Minor stylistic variance; all functionally equivalent. |

---

*Genesis 36: 5 disagreements logged. SOPHIA ruled with EMET/LOGOS on 3 items (vv. 2/14, 24, 35), with LOGOS/PNEUMA on 1 item (v. 6), and adopted a contextual approach on 1 item (hu Edom). PNEUMA was overruled on 3 items (vv. 2, 6, 35). LOGOS was overruled on 1 item (v. 24). Fewer disagreements than narrative chapters — genealogical material yields more translator convergence.*
